```python
# test_palindrome.py
# Replace `palindrome` with the actual module name where is_palindrome is defined.
import pytest

from palindrome import is_palindrome


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "text",
    [
        "racecar",
        "Madam",
        "A man a plan a canal Panama",
        "No lemon no melon",
        "Able was I ere I saw Elba",
    ],
)
def test_is_palindrome_returns_true_for_valid_palindromes(text):
    assert is_palindrome(text) is True


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "text",
    [
        "hello",
        "palindrome",
        "race a car",
        "A man a plan a canal Panamx",
    ],
)
def test_is_palindrome_returns_false_for_non_palindromes(text):
    assert is_palindrome(text) is False


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    ("text", "expected"),
    [
        ("", True),
        (" ", True),
        ("     ", True),
        ("a", True),
        (" a ", True),
        ("ab", False),
        ("Aa", True),
        ("a b a", True),
    ],
)
def test_is_palindrome_edge_cases(text, expected):
    assert is_palindrome(text) is expected


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    ("text", "expected"),
    [
        ("Madam, I'm Adam", False),
        ("Was it a car or a cat I saw?", False),
        ("a\tba", False),
        ("a\nba", False),
    ],
)
def test_is_palindrome_does_not_ignore_punctuation_or_non_space_whitespace(
    text,
    expected,
):
    # The implementation only lowercases and removes literal spaces: ' '
    assert is_palindrome(text) is expected


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    ("bad_input", "expected_exception"),
    [
        (None, AttributeError),
        (12321, AttributeError),
        (["r", "a", "c", "e", "c", "a", "r"], AttributeError),
        ({"text": "racecar"}, AttributeError),
        (b"racecar", TypeError),
    ],
)
def test_is_palindrome_raises_for_non_string_inputs(
    bad_input,
    expected_exception,
):
    with pytest.raises(expected_exception):
        is_palindrome(bad_input)
```
